channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Helsinki Airport restarts operations after temporary drone alert
item.description: On Friday, May 15, 2026, Helsinki Airport resumed its normal flight operations following a temporary suspension caused by a drone warning. The air traffic was paused from 4 a.m. to 7 a.m. after author...

item.title: Kirinyaga County Opens 21 Job Vacancies on Permanent and Contractual Terms
item.description: The Kirinyaga County Government, through its Public Service Board, has opened applications for 21 job positions across various departments. The vacancies include both permanent and pensionable roles a...

item.title: Annabella Sciorra's Acting Career and Impact in the 1990s
item.description: Annabella Sciorra, born on March 29, 1960, was a prominent figure in 1990s cinema, often recognized for her intelligent, grounded, and fiercely independent presence. She made her film debut with 'True...

item.title: Senate Sergeant-at-Arms assures fair treatment for Senator dela Rosa amid ICC warrant
item.description: Senate Sergeant-at-Arms Mao Aplasca confirmed that Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa will not receive any special treatment despite an International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant issued against hi...

item.title: Plaid Cymru Government in Cardiff Sets Out Plans for Expanded Welsh Powers
item.description: Following the 2026 Senedd election, Plaid Cymru has emerged as the largest party and is forming the next Welsh government, marking the first administration not led by Welsh Labour since devolution beg...

item.title: Federal Judge Criticizes Trump DOJ for Mishandling Discovery in Don Lemon Case
item.description: A federal magistrate judge sharply criticized the Trump administration's Department of Justice for mismanaging the discovery process in the criminal case involving former CNN reporter Don Lemon and pr...

item.title: Protecting Education Amid Increasing Extreme Weather in South Africa
item.description: South Africa is facing a growing challenge as extreme weather events, intensified by climate change, increasingly disrupt the basic education system. Recent extreme rainfall, particularly in the Easte...

item.title: Expert Warns Trump Could Use Presidential Emergency Directives to Influence 2026 Midterms
item.description: Former U.S. Special Envoy Jonathan Winer has expressed concern that President Donald Trump may employ Presidential Emergency Action Documents (PEADs) to influence the 2026 midterm elections. PEADs are...

item.title: Brian Keith Hosaka Reinstated as GCG Commissioner After ICI Tenure
item.description: Brian Keith Hosaka, who previously served as executive director and spokesperson of the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI), has officially returned as commissioner of the Governance Commi...

item.title: A Look at Shirley Jones’ Iconic Style and Stage Presence in the 1950s and 1960s
item.description: Shirley Mae Jones, born on March 31, 1934, is a celebrated American actress and singer whose career spans more than six decades. Known for her wholesome roles in musical films, she became a prominent ...

item.title: David Raya Close to Matching Arsenal’s Premier League Clean Sheet Record
item.description: Arsenal goalkeeper David Raya dey almost make club history as e dey just one clean sheet away from matching the all-time Premier League record for most shutouts in one season. According to ESPN report...

item.title: A Look at Everyday Life Through Humorous Vintage Photographs
item.description: This collection of vintage photographs captures amusing and candid moments of ordinary people from the 1920s to the 1950s. Unlike posed studio shots, these images reveal spontaneous, unplanned behavio...

item.title: Rigathi Gachagua Praises DCP Candidate's Strong Performance in Emurua Dikirr By-Election
item.description: In the recent Emurua Dikirr parliamentary by-election, the United Democratic Alliance's David Kipsang Keter emerged victorious with 18,266 votes, while the Democracy for the Citizens Party (DCP) candi...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Dismisses Sisisi Tolashe Amid Allegations of Misconduct
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has dismissed Sisisi Tolashe from her position as Minister of Social Development following a series of allegations concerning corruption, mismanagement, and nepotism within h...

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel faces criticism for private snorkeling visit at USS Arizona Memorial
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has come under scrutiny for taking a private snorkeling trip at the USS Arizona Memorial in Hawaii during August, without public disclosure. The memorial, which honors over 900...

item.title: Trial of Five Suspects in Senzo Meyiwa Murder Resumes in Pretoria High Court
item.description: The trial of the five men accused of murdering South African soccer icon Senzo Meyiwa resumed on Thursday at the Pretoria High Court. Meyiwa, who was the captain of Bafana Bafana, tragically lost his ...

item.title: PNP increases police patrols during scheduled brownouts to ensure public safety
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has announced that it is intensifying security operations across the country in response to potential crime risks during scheduled rotational brownouts. PNP Chief ...

item.title: Ombudsman Remulla criticizes Senator Dela Rosa for evading ICC arrest warrant
item.description: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla criticized Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa for allegedly evading an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court (ICC) related to alleged crimes against hu...

item.title: NEET UG 2026 Re-Examination Scheduled on June 21 Following Paper Leak Investigation
item.description: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has officially announced that the NEET UG 2026 examination will be re-conducted on Sunday, 21st June 2026. This decision follows the cancellation of the exam held on ...

item.title: Labour’s Purpose in 2026: Challenges for Leadership and Representation
item.description: The Guardian columnist Gaby Hinsliff explores the ongoing uncertainty within the Labour Party as potential leadership candidates prepare to challenge Keir Starmer. Wes Streeting’s dramatic resignation...
